headline: 9 core Ethereum L1 contracts + 1 Mantle L2 token discovered; governance token on Mantle L2

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search for 'mETH Protocol' and 'Mantle LSP' returned mETH token (0xd5f7838f5c461feff7fe49ea5ebaf7728bb0adfa) as primary contract with proxy pattern noted.
GitHub README at mantle-lsp/contracts serves as canonical deployed-contracts reference, listing 9 L1 Ethereum addresses and 1 L2 Mantle address with explicit role labels.

No separate audit PDF links found in protocol.audit_links field; audit references on website (Hexens, Secure3, MixBytes, QuantStamp, Verilog, BlockSec) link to docs.mantle.xyz/meth/security/audits (access blocked during assessment).
GitHub repo mantle-lsp/contracts contains /deployments and /broadcast directories; README provides definitive mainnet and L2 deployment table with addresses.

Protocol.chains=['Ethereum']; however, mETH is also bridged to Mantle L2 (0xcDA86A272531e8640cD7F1a92c01839911B90bb0). COOK governance token deployed on Mantle L2 (0x9f0c013016e8656bc256f948cd4b79ab25c7b94d). No Sepolia/testnet addresses in scope.
No factory pattern detected; all 9 L1 and 1 L2 contracts are singleton/core protocol instances. No enumeration views required.

Addresses classified per role descriptions in GitHub README: 'Staking' (entry point), 'METH'/'METHL2' (token), 'Oracle' (validation), 'OracleQuorumManager' (oracle interface), 'UnstakeRequestsManager' (queue), 'Pauser' (pause system), 'ReturnsAggregator' (fee/reward distribution), 'ConsensusLayerReturnsReceiver'/'ExecutionLayerReturnsReceiver' (reward receivers).
All 9 Ethereum L1 addresses and 1 Mantle L2 address trace to GitHub README and Etherscan search results; no snippet-only sightings.

rationale.verdict: Discovery slice catalogues 10 core protocol contracts (9 Ethereum L1, 1 Mantle L2 token) and identifies COOK governance token on Mantle. All addresses sourced from GitHub README, Etherscan, and CoinGecko/DeFiLlama. Downstream slices will verify control, upgradeability, and actor composition.

evidence.shows: Canonical contract address table in README listing all 9 L1 contracts (Staking, METH, Oracle, OracleQuorumManager, UnstakeRequestsManager, Pauser, ReturnsAggregator, ConsensusLayerReturnsReceiver, ExecutionLayerReturnsReceiver) and 1 L2 contract (METHL2).

mETH token page confirms contract is ERC-20 proxy using EIP-1967 Transparent Proxy pattern; implementation at 0x052f52748109bae13d6319a463d64b6a2a613e52; previously 0xc9173bf8bd5c1b071b5cae4122202a347b7eefab; total supply ~440k tokens; 9,699+ holders.

Official website identifies protocol as 'Institutional-Grade ETH Liquid Staking & Restaking'; references docs at docs.mantle.xyz/meth; mentions audit partners (Hexens, Secure3, MixBytes, QuantStamp, Verilog, BlockSec); states ~$5.2M invested in audits.

COOK governance token contract address 0x9f0c013016e8656bc256f948cd4b79ab25c7b94d (on Mantle network); protocol description confirms Ethereum L1 deployment governed by Mantle; cmETH token for restaking via EigenLayer/Symbiotic.

mETH described as value-accumulating ERC-20 receipt token for Mantle LSP; permissionless and non-custodial; pooled ETH staking with validator participation; reward distribution to stakers, LSP, and node operators.

unknowns: D2: deployed_contracts_doc URL (docs.mantle.xyz/meth) not directly fetchable; audit links blocked
D6: cmETH contract address for restaking not listed in primary GitHub README; appears in website copy but address not yet catalogued

protocol_metadata.about: Mantle Liquid Staking Protocol (LSP) is a permissionless, non-custodial ETH liquid staking protocol deployed on Ethereum L1 and governed by Mantle. Users stake ETH to receive mETH, a value-accumulating receipt token composable across DeFi. cmETH enables restaking via EigenLayer and Symbiotic. The protocol features a Guardian network, multi-oracle validation, and targeted ~24-hour redemptions via a Buffer Pool, aiming to deliver best-in-class institutional-grade staking.
